channel.title: A Field Guide to AI Fellowships
channel.description: Last 10 notes on A Field Guide to AI Fellowships

item.title: Programs Directory
item.description: Programs Directory The real programs, grouped by where they sit on the ladder.

item.title: Glossary
item.description: Glossary The jargon, demystified. This field loves acronyms and insider words; none of it is as hard as it sounds.

item.title: Reading and Courses
item.description: Reading and Courses Everything worth your time, nothing that isn’t.

item.title: Career and Communication Skills
item.description: Career and Communication Skills The meta-skills.

item.title: Research Skills
item.description: Research Skills The skills that separate “I can run experiments someone designed” from “I can figure out what’s worth studying and study it well.” These are what scientist-track fellowships are really selecting for, and they matter a lot for Interpretability and Alignment and AI Safety too.

item.title: Skills Map
item.description: Skills Map What you actually need to be good at, and which track needs which.

item.title: Alignment and AI Safety
item.description: Alignment and AI Safety In one line You work on making powerful AI systems reliably do what people intend — and not do catastrophic things — even as they get more capable.

item.title: Applied and Product ML
item.description: Applied and Product ML In one line You turn models into things people actually use — fine-tuning, evaluation for product use cases, data pipelines, shipping features.
